content.injection: Use Deku as an exhausted moving target and rescuer; he is not attending classes or living a normal student routine.

content.fact: Deku pushes himself past healthy limits during the solo campaign, degrading his judgment and appearance.
content.injection: Let exhaustion show through frayed speech, poor rest, tunnel vision, and worsening costume condition; do not make solo Deku look clean and refreshed.

entries.title: Deku Is Still Motivated By Rescue

context.label: Deku deteriorates through solo hero work

coordinates.label: One For All multiple Quirks active

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Even at his darkest, Deku remains driven by rescue and preventing harm rather than vengeance.
content.injection: Keep Deku’s core motive heroic; his flaw is self-destruction, not cruelty.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

content.fact: Deku defeats Muscular far more efficiently than in their first battle because his control and toolkit have grown.
content.injection: Use the Muscular rematch to show Deku’s growth and tactical restraint, not as a repeat of the summer camp fight.

content.fact: Deku’s solo campaign conflicts with his actual need for allies and shared burdens.

entries.title: Deku Refuses Comfort Until Forced To Stop

context.label: Deku deteriorates through solo hero work

coordinates.label: One For All multiple Quirks active

content.fact: Deku repeatedly pushes away help and comfort because stopping feels like letting others be hurt.
content.injection: Show self-denial as a concrete behavior: refusing food, rest, shelter, emotional honesty, and teamwork.

entries.title: Dark Hero Tone Should Stay Hopeful Under Strain

context.label: Deku deteriorates through solo hero work

coordinates.label: One For All multiple Quirks active

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The arc is grim, but Deku’s rescue instinct and his friends’ intervention keep it from becoming nihilistic.
content.injection: Do not turn Dark Hero into pure grimdark; the point is that heroism must be shared before it consumes Deku.
